<member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions">
            <summary>
            Represents metadata for a chat client agent, including its identifier, name, instructions, and description.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            This class is used to encapsulate information about a chat client agent, such as its unique
            identifier, display name, operational instructions, and a descriptive summary. It can be used to store and transfer
            agent-related metadata within a chat application.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Id">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent id.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Name">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent name.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Description">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent description.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatOptions">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the default chatOptions to use.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider">
--
            Creates a new instance of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions"/> with the same values as this instance.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions">
            <summary>
            Provides specialized run options for <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent"/> instances, extending the base agent run options with chat-specific configuration.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
===
            <summary>
            Logs a warning when <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.UseProvidedChatClientAsIs"/> is <see langword="true"/>
            and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence"/> is <see langword="true"/>,
            but no <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.PerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistingChatClient"/> is found in the custom chat client stack.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentLogMessages.LogAgentChatClientBackgroundResponseFallback(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.String,System.String)">
            <summary>
            Logs a warning when per-service-call persistence falls back to end-of-run persistence
            because the run involves background responses (continuation token resumption or
            <c>AllowBackgroundResponses</c>). Per-service-call persistence is
            unreliable in these scenarios because the caller may stop consuming the stream before
            the decorator's post-stream persistence code can execute.
--
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Id">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent id.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Name">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent name.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.Description">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the agent description.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatOptions">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the default chatOptions to use.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> instance to use for providing chat history for this agent.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.AIContextProviders">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the list of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIContextProvider"/> instances to use for providing additional context for each agent run.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.UseProvidedChatClientAsIs">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use the provided <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> instance as is,
            without applying any default decorators.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            By default the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent"/> applies decorators to the provided <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>
            for doing for example automatic function invocation. Setting this property to <see langword="true"/>
            disables adding these default decorators.
            Disabling is recommended if you want to decorate the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> with different decorators
            than the default ones. The provided <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> instance should then already be decorated
--
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ClearOnChatHistoryProviderConflict">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether to set the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent.ChatHistoryProvider"/> to <see langword="null"/>
            if the underlying AI service indicates that it manages chat history (for example, by returning a conversation id in the response), but a <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> is configured for the agent.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            Note that even if this setting is set to <see langword="false"/>, the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> will still not be used if the underlying AI service indicates that it manages chat history.
            </remarks>
            <value>
            Default is <see langword="true"/>.
            </value>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.WarnOnChatHistoryProviderConflict">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value indicating whether to log a warning if the underlying AI service indicates that it manages chat history
            (for example, by returning a conversation id in the response), but a <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ChatHistoryProvider"/> is configured for the agent.
            </summary>
            <value>
            Default is <see langword="true"/>.
            </value>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions.ThrowOnChatHistoryProviderConflict">
<exited with exit code 0>

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
</para>
            <para>
            When an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> is created via <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/>, this schema is automatically derived from the
            method's parameters using the configured <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> and <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            When no schema is specified, consuming chat clients should assume the "{}" or "true" schema, indicating that any JSON input is admissible.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema">
            <summary>Gets a JSON Schema describing the function's return value.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            When an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> is created via <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/>, this schema is automatically derived from the
            method's return type using the configured <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> and <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>.
            For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the schema is based on the
            unwrapped result type. Return schema generation can be excluded by setting
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/> to <see langword="true"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            A <see langword="null"/> value typically reflects a function that doesn't specify a return schema,
            a function that returns <see cref="T:System.Void"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>,
            or a function for which <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/> was set to <see langword="true"/>.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory">
            <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> class creates <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances that wrap .NET methods
            (specified as <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>). As part of this process, JSON schemas are
            automatically derived for both the function's input parameters (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>)
            and, by default, the function's return type (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>).
            These schemas are produced using the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> and
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>, and enable AI services to understand and
            interact with the function. Return value serialization and schema derivation behavior can be customized
            via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
            respectively.
            </para>
            </remarks>
            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
        </member>

                must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.

        <member name="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema">
            <summary>Gets a JSON Schema describing the function's return value.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            When an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> is created via <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/>, this schema is automatically derived from the
            method's return type using the configured <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> and <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>.
            For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the schema is based on the
            unwrapped result type. Return schema generation can be excluded by setting
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/> to <see langword="true"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            A <see langword="null"/> value typically reflects a function that doesn't specify a return schema,
            a function that returns <see cref="T:System.Void"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>,
            or a function for which <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/> was set to <see langword="true"/>.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory">
            <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> class creates <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances that wrap .NET methods
            (specified as <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>). As part of this process, JSON schemas are
            automatically derived for both the function's input parameters (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>)
            and, by default, the function's return type (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>).
            These schemas are produced using the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> and
            <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>, and enable AI services to understand and
            interact with the function. Return value serialization and schema derivation behavior can be customized
            via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
            respectively.
            </para>
            </remarks>
            <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
        </member>

        </member>

data.content: I see — `AIFunctionFactory.Create` takes a `Delegate` (single method) not an instance. I need to create functions for each method individually. Let me fix this by creating the tools list manually:

arguments.old_str:
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    instructions: """
        You are a research agent. When given a topic:
        1. Search the web for relevant information (perform multiple searches to cover different angles).
        2. Take notes on important findings using the note-taking tool.
        3. After gathering enough information, produce a final summary of your findings.
        Always cite which searches led to which findings.
        """,
    name: "ResearchAgent",
    description: "An agent that researches topics by searching the web and taking notes.",
    tools: [.. AIFunctionFactory.Create(tools)]);
arguments.new_str:
IList<AITool> aiTools = typeof(ResearchTools)
    .GetMethods(System.Reflection.BindingFlags.Public | System.Reflection.BindingFlags.Instance | System.Reflection.BindingFlags.DeclaredOnly)
    .Where(m => m.GetCustomAttributes(typeof(DescriptionAttribute), false).Length > 0)
    .Select(m => (AITool)AIFunctionFactory.Create(m, tools))
    .ToList();

var agent = new ChatClientAgent(
    chatClient,
    instructions: """
        You are a research agent. When given a topic:
        1. Search the web for relevant information (perform multiple searches to cover different angles).
        2. Take notes on important findings using the note-taking tool.
        3. After gathering enough information, produce a final summary of your findings.
        Always cite which searches led to which findings.
        """,
    name: "ResearchAgent",
    description: "An agent that researches topics by searching the web and taking notes.",
    tools: aiTools);

data.content:
The project builds cleanly. Here's what was built:

**ResearchAgent** — a .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework (`Microsoft.Agents.AI`) with two tools:

1. **`SearchWeb`** — queries DuckDuckGo's instant answer API and returns snippets
2. **`TakeNote`** — saves key findings for later display

The agent uses `ChatClientAgent` with streaming output, pinned to `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` with `Temperature = 0`. It accepts a custom topic via CLI args or defaults to "the impact of large language models on software development in 2025".

**To run:**
```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="your-key"
dotnet run --project ResearchAgent
# Or with a custom topic:
dotnet run --project ResearchAgent -- "quantum computing breakthroughs"
```
